auditFindings.finding: A fixed asset register containing identification numbers, acquisition dates, locations, and serial numbers was not maintained. Physical count of assets was not carried out at year-end.
auditFindings.recommendation: A complete and informative fixed asset register should be maintained for each asset and reconciled with the physical counts at year-end.

auditFindings.finding: Property, plant and equipment includes construction in progress with an aggregate balance of Birr 3,024,503 outstanding since June 2011 that had not been cleared.
auditFindings.recommendation: These balances should be investigated and appropriate action taken; they should not be carried forward indefinitely.

auditFindings.finding: Subsidiary ledger for details of expenditures incurred on imported goods was not maintained. A Cardiff elongated closed toilet seat was purchased through JV No. 010 at a total cost of Birr 1,170,333 for 190 American Standard items, resulting in a difference of Birr 6,877 between book and records.
auditFindings.recommendation: Subsidiary ledger for imported goods should be maintained and reconciled with book records.

auditFindings.finding: Trade receivable account includes long outstanding balances totalling Birr 19,779,675 (Associated Enterprises: Birr 17,994,458; Sundry debtors: Birr 10,151; AA Hilton share of profit: Birr 1,775,066). The total provision of Birr 18,004,609 is inadequate for the extent of write-off required.
auditFindings.recommendation: Long overdue receivables should be considered for provision and written off if there is no reasonable expectation of recovery, particularly for associated enterprises.

auditFindings.finding: There were differences in recording accrued liabilities: (a) the accrual for audit fee was not considered in full; (b) adjustment of Birr 119,000 following rejection of audit fees arrears by the tax office was not treated as the correct charge for the period.
auditFindings.recommendation: Accrued liabilities should be maintained correctly irrespective of the tax period.

auditFindings.finding: Lawyers' letters confirming the outcome of outstanding legal cases were not obtained, making it impossible to assess and provide for contingent liabilities.
auditFindings.recommendation: Lawyers' letters with detailed assessment of pending legal matters should be obtained as an important source to determine provisions for contingent liabilities.
